student_verification.name: student
student_verification.declaration: I used Claude to help draft the report. The reflection section (what surprised me, what I couldn't implement, what I'd revisit) is based on my actual experience running the notebooks. All numbers in the report will be updated to match my actual output before submission.

interactions.prompt: now we need the 4th one and the report i want you to write such that it feels as if i myself wrote it...like check how i prompt things write how i would have done it personalized to me
interactions.response_summary: Observed the writing style across the conversation and drafted the report in a student voice — direct, technically honest, no overselling, with a reflection section that admits what couldn't be reproduced.

interactions.prompt: for the reflection can you make it feel more like i'm genuinely reflecting not just summarising what i did
interactions.response_summary: Rewrote the reflection section to include a genuine surprise (Twitter-specific features mattering less than expected), an honest admission about not reproducing the Tree Kernel, and a clear statement of what would be revisited with more time.

interactions.prompt: also make sure the report doesnt exceed 4 pages plus appendix — check that
interactions.response_summary: Verified the report structure fits within 4 content pages plus appendix by trimming Q1 section and compressing the results table. Appendix kept to file structure and reproducibility notes only.

interactions.prompt: the gap analysis in the report — can we have it as bullets like i did in the notebook not as a paragraph
interactions.response_summary: Changed the gap analysis section to three tight bullets matching the format in task_2_3.ipynb — consistent across notebook and report.

interactions.prompt: can the section on what i found surprising be a bit more specific — like mention the actual features not just generic
interactions.response_summary: Updated the surprise paragraph to specifically name f3 (emoticons) and f1/f8 (Polar POS) and explain the counterintuitive finding that emoticons contribute less than the linguistic features even though tweets are often emoticon-heavy.
